name: Kettlebell Overhead Triceps Extension
resistance_profile: Free-weight resistance is hardest at the bottom where the elbows are fully flexed and the long head is stretched behind the head — a lengthened-biased stimulus.
cues: Cup the horns and keep the wrists stacked
Keep the elbows pointing forward, not flaring
execution_steps: Hold a single kettlebell by the horns with both hands, palms up, cupping the bell.
Press it overhead so the arms are straight and the upper arms frame the head.
Bend the elbows to lower the bell behind the head under control until you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Extend the elbows to return the bell overhead, keeping the upper arms still and vertical.
good_when: You want a lengthened-biased long-head builder with a kettlebell
You prefer the bell's offset load hanging behind the head for a deeper stretch
bad_when: Overhead loading bothers the shoulders or elbows
You lack the shoulder mobility to keep the upper arms vertical
common_errors: Flaring the elbows wide at the bottom
Letting the upper arms drift forward
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
regressions: Use a lighter bell or a single dumbbell
